§6Meteorite Compass§f:
The §6Meteorite Compass §f(appliedenergistics2:sky_compass), also called the §6Sky Compass§f, is a §6navigation tool §f that §bpoints to the nearest §6AE2 Meteorite §f in the world. It is the §6primary way §f to find §6Sky Stone §f and §6Charged Certus Quartz Ore §f in the overworld.

§6Production Path (misc/meteors_sky_compass.json)§f:
Pattern " a / aba /  a " (a 3x3 pattern with a hole in the corners):
- §ba§f: §6Iron Ingot §f(ingotIron) — 5 (top middle + all 4 sides + bottom middle)
- §bb§f: §6Charged Certus Quartz Crystal §f(appliedenergistics2:material:1) — 1 (centre)
- Yields 1× Meteorite Compass

§6Function (ItemSkyCompass.java)§f:
- §bDirection pointer §f: holds in hand, the compass needle points toward the §6nearest §fAE2 Meteorite
- §bDistance indicator §f: the compass shows §6distance §f to the meteorite via §6spin speed §f (faster = closer)
- §bY-level indicator §f: the compass points §6down §f if the meteorite is below and §6up §f if above
- §bNo power §f: the compass is §6passive §f — no power or fuel needed
- §bY-level support §f: works across the §6overworld §f, §6Nether §f, and most §6mod dimensions §f

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 0 items the Meteorite Compass is a component of, but it is referenced in 2 §6use sites §f across the mod pack. Common uses:
- §bFinding meteorites §f: locate §6AE2 Meteorites §f in the overworld
- §bCharged Certus Quartz §f: meteorites are the §6primary source §f of §6Charged Certus Quartz Ore §f

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: a §6Charged Certus Quartz Crystal §f is the §6key ingredient §f — craft one from a §6Certus Quartz Crystal §f in a §6Charger §f
- §eTip§f: hold the compass in your §6main hand §f and the needle will point to the §6nearest §f meteorite
- §eTip§f: a §6fast-spinning §f compass means the meteorite is §bclose §f; a §6slow §f compass means §bfar §f
- §cWarning§f: the compass §cdoes not §f work if there is §bno §f meteorite within §6render distance §f
- §cWarning§f: a meteorite is §6always §f at least §b20-30 §f blocks below the surface; dig down to find it
